public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ritual attendant.
appearance: Ritual garments, symbolic tokens, and carefully maintained devotional detail make the role legible at a glance.
manner: Balances comfort with correction, sounding gentle until rite, taboo, or consequence is threatened. In Shirsh, that reads as someone investigative, magically literate, and discreet, balancing inquiry with regulation, and the habits of a ritual attendant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ritual attendant in and around Shirshal, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Shirshal workable through the obligations of ritual attendant while managing the immediate pressure of magical law violations in Shirsh.
hidden_pressure: Must ensure that rite remains trustworthy even when civic pressure tries to convert it into mere administration. Around Shirshal, that usually means carrying magical law violations, magical compliance, institutional scrutiny without letting the role of ritual attendant visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Veyn Veilthorn needs discreet aid to prevent a ritual lapse, desecration, or public crisis of trust around Shirshal.
Veyn Veilthorn may ask the party to retrieve someone, something, or some truth before a rite can proceed safely.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Ritual dress, offering marks, and devotional cadence make Veyn Veilthorn immediately legible as a spiritual intermediary. In Shirshal, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Magi Magistratus.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Shirshal: shrines, cathedrals, funerary halls, or ritual precincts

first_impression: Veyn Veilthorn presents as Balances comfort with correction, sounding gentle until rite, taboo, or consequence is threatened. In Shirsh, that reads as someone investigative, magically literate, and discreet, balancing inquiry with regulation, and the habits of a ritual attendant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Ritual Attendant, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Shirsh.
what_they_refuse: Veyn Veilthorn refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Ritual Attendant too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Veyn Veilthorn can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Veyn Veilthorn adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Veyn Veilthorn is Known locally through day-to-day work as ritual attendant. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Ritual Attendant.
